headline: Pillars of nuclear non-proliferation treaty are being undermined
description: The 11th Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, held every five years, is set to begin Monday in New York in a tense atmosphere. In addition to the war waged against Iran by the US and Israel, all pillars of the treaty have become fragile.

Man who planned shooting at Washington press gala targeted Trump administration officials, authorities say
description: The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, who is set to appear in court as early as Monday, wrote a manifesto in which he expressed his anger toward the government and mocked the event's security measures.

headline: Charles III visits the US in bid to repair the 'special relationship' between London and Washington
description: The British royal couple is expected in the US capital on Monday for a four-day visit, as tensions have mounted between the two countries.

headline: Vincent Lemire, historian: 'In the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, Jerusalem is not the obstacle, it could be the solution'
description: The historian suggests focusing on the concrete living conditions of Jerusalem's residents in order to invent new forms of coexistence between Israelis and Palestinians.

headline: Oil traders' windfall profits highlight paradoxes of Swiss model
description: Geneva is full of rumors about the record profits made by oil market players. The legal framework in which they operate has drawn criticism and sparked calls for tighter regulation.

'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
description: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

headline: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
description: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

headline: French presidential election: Who's already running, who could be, and key dates
